steps.title: Find the main claim
steps.content: The narrator says it was fortunate that Amos Barton did not overhear his neighbors. The following question generalizes that idea: people would not be happier comparing their self-image with others' views of them.

steps.title: Reject unrelated ideas
steps.content: A focuses on setbacks to virtue, B claims people fixate on approval, and D discusses truthfulness about others' flaws. The text's main point is that ignorance of others' judgments can be beneficial. The answer is C.
